violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the establishment Certified Food Safety Manager. Per the PIC, Food Safety Class has been successfully completed, Certified Food Safety Manager License Application to be filled out and submitted to RIDOH Center for Food Protection as soon as possible. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The high temp dish machine was observed not reaching proper high temperature during the rinse cycle to achieve proper sanitization. Per the PIC, all utensils/equipment are properly sanitized in the 3-bay sink quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served as needed. Employee(s) is to be enrolled in a class and certified in food safety as soon as possible. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the establishment Certified Food Safety Manager. Employee to be certified in food safety and registered with the RIDOH CFP as soon as possible. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The milk chest cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps at 41 to 46 degrees. Use of the unit for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until serviced/repaired. - The Open-Air coolers were observed maintaining ambient temperature of 56-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the unit for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until serviced/repaired. - The faucet to the wash bay of the 3 bay sink was observed leaking/dripping while turned off. - The high temp dish machine was observed not reaching proper high temperature during the rinse cycle to achieve proper sanitization. All utensils/equipment are to be properly sanitized in the 3 bay sink quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served as needed. Employee(s) is to be enrolled in a class and certified in food safety as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the establishment Certified Food Safety Manager. Employee to be certified in food safety and registered with the RIDOH CFP as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. All equipment/utensils are to be properly sanitized in the kitchen 3 bay sink using the wall mounted Quaternary Ammonium solution until dish machine is adjusted/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

Cut Chicken Patties and corn/Bean Salad was observed in the milk chest cooler at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

Milk was observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit in the open-air cooler near the register. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk was moved back to the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The milk chest cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps at 41 to 46 degrees. Use of the unit for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until serviced/repaired. - The Open-Air coolers were observed maintaining ambient temperature of 56-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the unit for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until serviced/repaired. - The faucet to the wash bay of the 3 bay sink was observed leaking/dripping while turned off. - The high temp dish machine was observed not reaching proper high temperature during the rinse cycle to achieve proper sanitization. All utensils/equipment are to be properly sanitized in the 3 bay sink quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermo-label (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. During the inspection, the machine was tested 7-8 times and the observed highest temperature achieved during the rinse cycle was 123 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC is to ensure all utensils/equipment are to be sanitized with chemical sanitizer at the 3-bay sink until serviced/repaired. Service to be called. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
Frozen item(s) were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
The door gasket to the walk-in cooler was observed to be torn and not in good repair. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/12/2025

At the time of inspection, hot and cold water were not available at the Dish Room Hand Sink. A HANDWASHING SINK shall be equipped to provide water at a temperature of at least 100 degrees Fahrenheit through a mixing valve or combination faucet. PIC to make school maintenance aware for service/adjustment so hot water is available at the dish room hand sink. (Per the PIC, a work order is in place.) Per the PIC, the hand sink nearby outside the dishwashing room is currently being used while the hand sink in the dish area is out of service. (Spoke with School Dept. Maintenance personnel on site at the time of re-inspection about repairs needed.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

At the time of inspection, the Open Air Coolers were all observed to be out of service for storage of milk. Per the PIC, milk is only placed in cooler during time of breakfast and lunch periods, and immediately placed back in the walk-in cooler between periods. Per the PIC, technician was called for service. Technician arrived at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2024

At the time of inspection, hot and cold water were not available at the Dish Room Hand Sink. A HANDWASHING SINK shall be equipped to provide water at a temperature of at least 100 degrees Fahrenheit through a mixing valve or combination faucet. PIC to make school maintenance aware for service/adjustment so hot water is available at the dish room hand sink. (Per the PIC, a work order is in place.) Per the PIC, the hand sink nearby outside the dishwashing room is currently being used while the hand sink in the dish area is out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

Milk being cold held in the open air cooler was observed held at 54-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the milk was stored in the open-air cooler overnight. Milks were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

At the time of inspection, the open-air cooler with milk stored in it was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment. The unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 47 to 56 degrees Fahrenheit with milk observed at 54-56 degrees Fahrenheit in the bottom shelf area. Use of the unit for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until service/repaired/replaced. (Currently, per the PIC, the 2 other open-air coolers are NOT IN USE due to improper temperature control.) New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2024

At the time of inspection, hot water was not available at the Dish Room Hand Sink. A HANDWASHING SINK shall be equipped to provide water at a temperature of at least 100 degrees Fahrenheit through a mixing valve or combination faucet. PIC to make school maintenance aware for service/adjustment so hot water is available at the dish room hand sink. (Per the PIC, a work order is in place.) New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

At the time of inspection, hot water was not available at the Dish Room Hand Sink. A HANDWASHING SINK shall be equipped to provide water at a temperature of at least 100 degrees Fahrenheit through a mixing valve or combination faucet. PIC to make school maintenance aware for service/adjustment so hot water is available at the dish room hand sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The two open air coolers closest to the ware-washing area were observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Open air cooler to only be used for the storage of shelf stable drinks until it can be serviced/replaced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2023
